title: FAIL: Wrong hemisphere flag for Sirius (σ₁=+1 instead of -1)
_fixture_purpose: FAIL fixture: declares σ₁=+1 for Sirius (dec=-16.7°) observed from lat=51.5°N. Since lat>0 and dec<0, the correct flag is σ₁=-1 (opposite hemispheres). This is the most dangerous error in RT celestial nav — wrong orientation makes above-horizon stars appear below, and vice versa.

witnesses.label: Sirius WRONG σ₁
witnesses._note: WRONG: σ₁=+1 but lat=51.5>0, dec=-16.7<0 → opposite hemispheres → σ₁=-1

witnesses.label: Rigel WRONG σ₁
witnesses._note: WRONG: same error, σ₁=+1 for opposite-hemisphere star

fail_ledger.reason: σ₁=+1 for southern-hemisphere stars observed from northern latitude. Must be σ₁=-1 when lat and dec have opposite signs.
